stages.output: Audit-ready decision record and evidence trail for the portfolio file.

specificContent.reviewMoment: Ahead of insurance renewal and internal asset risk update

professionalWorkup.primaryQuestion: What physical-risk evidence and caveats should the reviewer consider before deciding whether the current portfolio assumption can remain unchanged?
professionalWorkup.summary: Review package across 5 selected sources: source suitability, spatial and temporal context, resilience gaps, caveats, and reviewer questions.

sourceSuitability.name: External Data Centre Physical Risk Signal
sourceSuitability.caveat: Useful as context and trigger, not as final site-level evidence.

sourceSuitability.name: Screening-level Spatial Hazard Context
sourceSuitability.sourceType: Public or local screening-level hazard-context layer
sourceSuitability.caveat: Needs asset-location confidence, source-scale caveats, and site-level evidence before conclusions.

sourceSuitability.name: Standard Physical-risk Documentation Assumption
sourceSuitability.caveat: A record status, not independent evidence.

sourceSuitability.name: Partial Site Resilience Note
sourceSuitability.caveat: Missing evidence in the record does not prove absence of controls.

assetGeometry.locationConfidence: demo coordinate; not a real site assessment

operations: Represent the asset as a review geometry and record location confidence.
Classify every source by native spatial scale and intended use.
Choose the appropriate spatial operation: point-in-polygon, buffer overlay, nearest grid cell, or scale caveat review.
Turn spatial findings into reviewer questions and caveats instead of a site-level risk conclusion.

sourceDiagnostics.name: External Data Centre Physical Risk Signal
sourceDiagnostics.caveat: Market or regional signal cannot clear or fail a single site.

sourceDiagnostics.name: Screening-level Spatial Hazard Context
sourceDiagnostics.reviewUse: screening-level local context and reviewer questions
sourceDiagnostics.confidence: medium for triage, low for site clearance
sourceDiagnostics.caveat: Native scale and asset-location confidence must be recorded before any human conclusion.

sourceDiagnostics.name: Standard Physical-risk Documentation Assumption
sourceDiagnostics.reviewUse: business context and assumption to test
sourceDiagnostics.caveat: Internal record is not independent spatial evidence.

sourceDiagnostics.name: Partial Site Resilience Note
sourceDiagnostics.caveat: Missing records do not prove missing controls.

sourceDiagnostics.name: Data Centre Continuity Dependency Note
sourceDiagnostics.reviewUse: power, cooling, water, access, and BI relevance
sourceDiagnostics.caveat: Operational context is not verified site performance.

spatialTemporalContext.professionalBoundary: This is a geospatial review note, not a GIS-certified site assessment or engineering conclusion.

hazardEvidenceInterpretation.whyItMatters: Data-centre downtime can be driven by flooded plant, access routes, switchgear, drainage failure, or low-lying critical equipment.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.evidenceRequest: Ask for site drainage design, floor levels, freeboard, critical equipment elevation, barriers, access-road floodability, and maintenance records.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.boundary: This supports review scoping; it does not certify flood protection or clear the site.

hazardEvidenceInterpretation.whyItMatters: Heat stress can affect cooling capacity, server inlet temperature, equipment derating, and emergency procedures.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.evidenceRequest: Ask for cooling redundancy, ASHRAE / OEM operating limits, server inlet temperature thresholds, cooling ride-through, and loss-of-cooling procedures.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.boundary: This supports review questions; it does not validate thermal design or engineering resilience.

hazardEvidenceInterpretation.whyItMatters: Grid interruption, switchgear exposure, generator performance, fuel logistics, and ATS / UPS configuration can drive outage risk.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.evidenceRequest: Ask for utility feeds, UPS configuration, ATS and switchgear records, generator capacity, fuel duration, test logs, and single-point-of-failure review.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.boundary: This supports review evidence preparation; it does not prove uptime, redundancy class, or loss estimate.

hazardEvidenceInterpretation.whyItMatters: Cooling systems may depend on water availability, chiller performance, cooling-tower operation, and local operating restrictions.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.evidenceRequest: Ask for WUE, water source, backup water plan, cooling-tower demand, drought restriction exposure, and cooling contingency procedure.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.boundary: This supports dependency review preparation; it does not validate operational readiness.

hazardEvidenceInterpretation.whyItMatters: Storms can affect roof and envelope integrity, yard equipment, utility feeds, access, fuel replenishment, and supplier continuity.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.evidenceRequest: Ask for roof and envelope inspection, yard-equipment anchorage, storm-response plan, backup fuel logistics, and open risk-engineering recommendations.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.boundary: This supports review preparation; it does not create an engineering conclusion.

hazardEvidenceInterpretation.whyItMatters: The same physical trigger may matter differently depending on SLA exposure, redundancy, customers, cloud region role, and recovery assumptions.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.evidenceRequest: Ask for SLA exposure, RTO / RPO, customer criticality, dependency mapping, contingency plan, and internal BI assumptions.
hazardEvidenceInterpretation.boundary: This supports reviewer context; it does not estimate BI, MFL, PML, or pricing.

resilienceEvidenceGaps.label: Site and asset basis
resilienceEvidenceGaps.neededEvidence: Location basis, campus boundary or asset point, IT load, rack density, and critical dependencies.

resilienceEvidenceGaps.label: Flood and surface-water controls
resilienceEvidenceGaps.neededEvidence: Floor levels, drainage design, critical equipment elevation, flood barriers, access-road exposure, and maintenance records.

resilienceEvidenceGaps.label: Power continuity
resilienceEvidenceGaps.neededEvidence: Utility feeds, UPS, ATS, switchgear, standby generators, fuel duration, and test logs.

resilienceEvidenceGaps.label: Cooling continuity
resilienceEvidenceGaps.neededEvidence: Chiller / CRAH / CRAC redundancy, heat-stress limits, cooling ride-through, and loss-of-cooling procedures.

resilienceEvidenceGaps.label: Water dependency
resilienceEvidenceGaps.neededEvidence: Water source, WUE, cooling-tower demand, drought restrictions, and backup water plan.

resilienceEvidenceGaps.label: Risk-engineering record
resilienceEvidenceGaps.neededEvidence: Inspection date, open recommendations, evidence owner, and next review date.

reviewQuestions: Which site-level evidence is needed before the current portfolio assumption can be kept unchanged?
Which external signals are only suitable as review triggers rather than asset-level evidence?
Which hazards need follow-up: flood / surface water, heat, wind, water dependency, power, cooling, or BI?
Which caveats must be recorded before this case is closed?
Who should own the next evidence request: asset owner, broker, risk engineering, or portfolio team?
professionalWorkup.professionalBoundary: No validated site-level risk, underwriting, pricing, valuation, insurability, Tier / standards compliance, BI, MFL / PML, or engineering conclusion has been made.

executionPlan.primaryNextStep: Request current site-level resilience evidence and refer to risk engineering if evidence remains incomplete.

evidenceRequestPolicy.currentWorkflowAction: Create the primary site_asset_basis request before modeled human review; remaining evidence tasks stay available for follow-up routing.

blockers.title: Site evidence is incomplete
blockers.reason: The file does not yet contain current site-level resilience evidence.

blockers.title: External signal cannot clear the asset
blockers.reason: External or screening-level signals can trigger review, but cannot support site clearance.

blockers.title: Decision boundary must stay human-owned
blockers.reason: The file prepares evidence and caveats; it does not make underwriting, pricing, engineering, or insurability decisions.

handoffActions.label: Request site-level evidence
handoffActions.description: Request current site-level resilience evidence and refer to risk engineering if evidence remains incomplete.

handoffActions.label: Refer to risk engineering
handoffActions.description: Refer to risk engineering for decision-grade engineering assessment if evidence remains incomplete.

handoffActions.label: Keep under review with caveat
handoffActions.description: Keep the asset under review when closure depends on additional evidence or committee review.

retryPolicy.retryReason: Evidence owner has not returned the required site-level evidence.

escalationPath.action: Escalate missing evidence and keep the case open.
Review whether a caveated decision record is acceptable.

exceptionPath.condition: Evidence request remains open after SLA.
Reviewer keeps the case open with a caveat.

supportingDetails.title: Source caveats
items: External Data Centre Physical Risk Signal: Useful as context and trigger, not as final site-level evidence.
Screening-level Spatial Hazard Context: Needs asset-location confidence, source-scale caveats, and site-level evidence before conclusions.
Standard Physical-risk Documentation Assumption: A record status, not independent evidence.
Partial Site Resilience Note: Missing evidence in the record does not prove absence of controls.
Data Centre Continuity Dependency Note: Explains relevance, not site performance.

supportingDetails.title: Hazard translation
items: Flood / surface water: Ask for site drainage design, floor levels, freeboard, critical equipment elevation, barriers, access-road floodability, and maintenance records.
Extreme heat: Ask for cooling redundancy, ASHRAE / OEM operating limits, server inlet temperature thresholds, cooling ride-through, and loss-of-cooling procedures.
Power continuity: Ask for utility feeds, UPS configuration, ATS and switchgear records, generator capacity, fuel duration, test logs, and single-point-of-failure review.
Cooling / water dependency: Ask for WUE, water source, backup water plan, cooling-tower demand, drought restriction exposure, and cooling contingency procedure.
Wind / storm: Ask for roof and envelope inspection, yard-equipment anchorage, storm-response plan, backup fuel logistics, and open risk-engineering recommendations.
Business interruption context: Ask for SLA exposure, RTO / RPO, customer criticality, dependency mapping, contingency plan, and internal BI assumptions.

supportingDetails.title: Spatial method
items: External Data Centre Physical Risk Signal: scale caveat review; Market or regional signal cannot clear or fail a single site.
Screening-level Spatial Hazard Context: point / buffer overlay; Native scale and asset-location confidence must be recorded before any human conclusion.
Standard Physical-risk Documentation Assumption: assumption context review; Internal record is not independent spatial evidence.
Partial Site Resilience Note: site evidence gap extraction; Missing records do not prove missing controls.
Data Centre Continuity Dependency Note: dependency context mapping; Operational context is not verified site performance.
